data.text: Asia prices of LPG, naphtha jump after Saudi terminal outage
data.snippet: Prices of liquefied petroleum gas and naphtha jumped in Asia on Thursday after Saudi Aramco halted exports from a key terminal, disrupting supplies to the region, according to trade sources and LSEG data, with India likely to take the biggest hit.

image.text: A view shows Saudi Aramco's Juaymah facility, Saudi Arabia November 26, 2013.

data.text: Aramco cancels Juaymah LPG deliveries through March for repairs, traders say
data.snippet: Saudi Aramco's Juaymah liquefied petroleum gas export terminal is expected to be offline for repairs for at least a month after an outage this week forced the cancellation of cargoes through March, traders said on Thursday.

image.text: A satellight image shows smoke rising from the area in Al Juaymah, Saudi Arabia, February 25, 2026.

data.text: Spain's grid operator Redeia to invest $7.7 billion through 2029
data.snippet: Spain's Redeia, the owner of power grid operator Red Electrica, plans to invest 6.5 billion euros ($7.67 billion) through 2029, it said on Thursday, mostly to expand and upgrade the country's power network.

image.text: The logo of Spanish electricity grid operator Redeia at its headquarters in Madrid, Spain, October 28, 2025.

relatedHeadlines.text: Portugal's EDP moves to supply data centre boom in Iberia, CEO says
relatedHeadlines.snippet: Portugal's largest utility EDP is moving to supply the boom in data centre projects in Iberia, attracted by electricity prices that are set to remain low compared with much of Europe, CEO Miguel Stilwell de Andrade said on Thursday.

relatedHeadlines.text: Spanish grid blackout risks remain, Endesa CEO says
relatedHeadlines.snippet: Spain and Portugal's widespread blackout last year was at risk of being repeated if similar conditions arose, the CEO of utility Endesa said on Thursday, blaming the energy mix planning of Spanish power grid operator REE for the outage.

data.text: Engie shares surge on $14 billion UK power grid deal
data.snippet: Shares in French utility Engie surged on Thursday after it said it would buy major British grid company UK Power Networks for $14 billion, marking its largest ever deal and a return to acquisitions after several years of selling off non-core assets.

image.text: The Engie logo is displayed during the 107th session of the Congress of Mayors organised by the "France's Mayors' Association" (AMF) at the Paris Expo Porte de Versailles convention center in Paris, France, November 19, 2025.

relatedHeadlines.text: Belgium wants more nuclear power production, Engie's CFO says
relatedHeadlines.snippet: The Belgian government is pushing to develop new nuclear energy capacity and to extend the lifespan of existing reactors, according to the chief financial officer of the reactors' operator, France's Engie, which has warned it has little interest in maintaining nuclear assets.

relatedHeadlines.text: BREAKINGVIEWS-Li clan amps up quest for a simple sprawl discount
relatedHeadlines.snippet: Victor Li is trying to wheel and deal his way back to a plain old conglomerate discount. The son of billionaire Li Ka-shing, one of Asia's original tycoons, agreed to offload electricity distributor UK Power Networks to French utility Engie for $14.3 billion. It frees up capital for new investment, but finding better options than returning it to shareholders will be tough.

data.text: Italy's Eni beats profit forecast on strong upstream business
data.snippet: Italian energy group Eni beat forecasts on Thursday with a 35% year-on-year jump in fourth-quarter adjusted earnings, driven by a strong performance in its exploration and production division and improved refining results.

image.text: The logo of Italian multinational energy company Eni is displayed at their booth during the LNG 2023 energy trade show in Vancouver, British Columbia, Canada, July 12, 2023.

relatedHeadlines.text: Eni to reach final investment decision for Indonesia gas projects next month, regulator says
relatedHeadlines.snippet: Indonesia's upstream oil and gas regulator SKK Migas expects Italy's Eni to reach its final investment decision next month for the development of the Geng North and Gendalo-Gendang gas projects, its head Djoko Siswanto told reporters.

relatedHeadlines.text: Italy's Eni weighs return to oil and gas trading, FT reports
relatedHeadlines.snippet: Italian energy major Eni is weighing re-entering oil and gas trading as it seeks the outsized returns enjoyed by BP, Shell and TotalEnergies, amid geopolitically-driven price volatility, the Financial Times reported on Wednesday.

data.text: Siemens Energy should not 'squander' wind division, investor Deka says
data.snippet: Siemens Energy should not sell its wind division below value, a top-20 shareholder said on Thursday at the group's annual general meeting, where the future of the struggling business will be a key topic after calls for a spin-off.

image.text: A logo is seen at Siemens Energy's site in Muelheim an der Ruhr, Germany, August 3, 2022.

relatedHeadlines.text: Siemens Energy should not 'squander' wind division, top-20 investor says
relatedHeadlines.snippet: Siemens Energy should not sell its wind division below value, a top-20 shareholder said on Thursday at the group's annual general meeting, where the future of the struggling business will be a key topic after calls for a spin-off.

relatedHeadlines.text: Activist fund Ananym pushes Siemens Energy to spin off ailing wind division
relatedHeadlines.snippet: U.S.-based activist investor Ananym Capital has taken a stake in German power equipment manufacturer Siemens Energy and is asking the group's management to review its loss-making wind division, its co-founder said on Tuesday.

data.text: Shell inspects subsea equipment at curtailed Ormen Lange gas field
data.snippet: Shell is currently troubleshooting parts of the subsea system at its offshore Ormen Lange gas field in the Norwegian Sea, where output has been curtailed since February 16, the company said on Thursday.

image.text: A view shows a logo of Shell petrol station in South East London, Britain, February 2, 2023.

data.text: UK's Drax shares hit 20-year high as power firm beats profit estimates
data.snippet: British power company Drax beat annual profit expectations and raised its dividend on Thursday, driven by strong pellet output in North America and record renewable generation, lifting shares to a near two-decade high.

image.text: Signage is seen on the side of a train delivering biomass fuel pellets at the Drax Power station near Selby, Britain, December 13, 2024.

relatedHeadlines.text: Sempra lifts five-year spending plan, beats quarterly profit estimates
relatedHeadlines.snippet: Energy infrastructure company Sempra raised its five-year capital plan by 16% and beat Wall Street estimates for fourth-quarter adjusted profit on Thursday, helped by strong performance at its Texas utility.

relatedHeadlines.text: Vistra beats quarterly core profit estimates on AI-driven power demand
relatedHeadlines.snippet: Power producer Vistra Corp on Thursday beat Wall Street expectations for fourth-quarter adjusted core profit, as an AI-driven surge in electricity demand from data centers fueled earnings.

relatedHeadlines.text: Utility PSEG forecasts 2026 profit below estimates, raises spending plan
relatedHeadlines.snippet: Public Service Enterprise Group on Thursday forecast annual profit below analyst expectations, but still raised its five-year capital spending plan as it ramps up investments in grid modernization amid surging power demand.

data.text: US natgas futures hit five-month low on milder weather forecasts, low storage draw
data.snippet: U.S. natural gas futures eased to a five-month low on Thursday on a small weekly storage withdrawal and forecasts for milder weather and lower heating demand over the next two weeks than previously expected.

image.text: A vertical gas flaring furnace is seen in Ughelli, Delta State, Nigeria September 16, 2020.

relatedHeadlines.text: POLL-US natural gas stockpiles likely fell by 55 billion cubic feet last week
FACTBOX-US natgas end-of-season storage seen at four-year low in March
relatedHeadlines.snippet: U.S. natural gas storage is on track to end the November 2025-March 2026 winter withdrawal season at a four-year low of 1.790 trillion cubic feet (tcf) on March 31, 2026, according to analysts' estimates.

relatedHeadlines.text: US natgas prices at Waha Hub in Texas in negative territory for record 12th day
relatedHeadlines.snippet: U.S. spot natural gas prices for Monday at the Waha Hub in the Permian Shale in West Texas closed in negative territory for a record 12th time in a row as pipeline constraints trap gas in the nation's biggest oil-producing basin, forcing some energy firms to pay others to take gas associated with their oil production.

data.text: ROI-Africa primed for solar breakthrough after record capacity growth: Maguire
data.snippet: The continent of Africa looks set to emerge as a key driver of global solar power production over the rest of the 2020s thanks to a potent mix of policy support, rapid economic growth and declining component costs.

image.text: A view shows solar panels at the green hydrogen proof-of-concept site in Vredendal, Western Cape, South Africa, November 15, 2022.

relatedHeadlines.text: ROI-Old-economy demands may stall emerging market decarbonization: Maguire
relatedHeadlines.snippet: While North America and Europe scramble to build the data centers needed to run 21st-century businesses, many emerging markets remain anchored to 20th-century products like steel, cement and plastics for job creation and economic growth.

data.text: Ukraine's Naftogaz to deliver US LNG via Klaipeda terminal
data.snippet: Ukraine’s state oil and gas company Naftogaz will deliver U.S. liquefied natural gas to Ukraine via Lithuania's Klaipeda terminal for the first time, the firm said on Thursday.

image.text: A view shows the logo of the Ukrainian state energy company Naftogaz outside its headquarters in central Kiev, Ukraine December 2, 2019.

data.text: Sweden tells energy sector to raise security, but faces no specific threat
data.snippet: Sweden has told its energy industry to raise security levels following a recent cyberattack on Polish infrastructure, although this was not in response to a specific threat, the Nordic country's signal intelligence agency said on Thursday.

relatedHeadlines.text: NORDIC POWER-Forward prices fall to over one-week low on wet weather view
relatedHeadlines.snippet: Nordic forward power prices fell to a more than one-week low on Thursday, pressured by a milder weather outlook and weaker German power prices.

data.text: Floating wind market 'much smaller' than expected, Technip chief says
data.snippet: Demand for floating wind turbines is much lower than imagined only three to five years ago, Technip Energies CEO Arnaud Pieton said on Thursday, reflecting broader concerns across the wind power industry.

relatedHeadlines.text: US freezes five big offshore wind projects, shares dive
relatedHeadlines.snippet: The Trump administration suspended leases on Monday for five large offshore wind projects that are under construction off the U.S. East Coast over what it called national security concerns, sending shares of offshore wind companies plunging.

data.text: Hanwha and Greek ΟΝΕΧ Shipyards to expand activities in the US
data.snippet: Greek ΟΝΕΧ Shipyards and Technology said on Thursday it signed an agreement with South Korean Hanwha Power Systems to expand their joint activities in the United States in shipbuilding, energy production and regasification units.

data.text: EUROPE GAS-Prices rangebound on stable supply, warmer temperatures
data.snippet: Benchmark Dutch and British wholesale gas prices traded in a narrow range on Thursday morning as warmer weather across the continent softened demand, while market participants closely monitored US-Iran nuclear talks.

image.text: A gas cooker is seen in Boroughbridge, northern England November 13, 2012.

relatedHeadlines.text: EUROPE POWER-Further gains in renewable energy weigh on spot prices
relatedHeadlines.snippet: European spot electricity contracts for Friday dropped on the expectation that demand will continue to fall while renewable generation is set to rise.

notifications.message: Your requested language with value en-GB is not supported, using en-us instead.
